update_process: When a new fork-owned area is introduced, add a rule here before the next upstream sync.
Run `python scripts/validate_sync_policy.py` in CI and before merging sync automation changes.
Prefer the narrowest rule that explains ownership; use `manual-review` until a stable strategy is proven.

rules.name: brand-and-launch-assets
rules.reason: Fork identity, README positioning, launch site, and GPT-image assets must survive upstream refreshes unchanged unless the fork intentionally rebrands again.

rules.name: fork-home-aliases-and-entrypoints
rules.reason: CLI aliases, fork-home environment variables, and startup wrappers must absorb upstream behavior while retaining Tota/Hermes-Turbo entrypoints.

rules.name: profile-distribution-and-warmup-surface
rules.reason: Desktop and car profile logic is fork-owned behavior layered on top of upstream CLI/runtime changes.

rules.name: llm-project-mapper-core-onboarding
rules.reason: Project mapping is a fork policy and should not be dropped by an upstream sync.

rules.name: upstream-owned-core-and-site
rules.reason: General upstream surfaces should default to Hermes unless another narrower rule overrides them.

rules.name: benchmark-measurements-and-derived-assets
rules.reason: Benchmark JSON is the source of truth; markdown, battle cards, PDFs, and stale markers must be regenerated only from measured output after sync.

rules.name: performance-patches-and-budgets
rules.reason: Fast-path patches and performance budgets are intentionally divergent; every upstream sync must review them against the latest measured runtime behavior.
